name: Chocolate-Mint Cookie Sandwiches
recipeIngredient: 3 ounces Unsweetened baking chocolate (melted and cooled)
recipeInstructions:
Recipe by: The Joy of Cookies, pp. 156-7
1. To prepare cookies: In a medium bowl, combine flour, baking powder and salt; set aside. In a mixing bowl, beat butter, sugar and vanilla until light and fluffy. Add egg, then melted chocolate, beating well after each addition. Stir in the flour mixture (about half a cup at a time), blending well after each addition. Divide dough into two halves. Form each half into a log that is 9 inches long and about 2 inches in diameter. Wrap in plastic and freeze or refrigerate until firm, 1 to 4 hours.
2. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Grease baking sheets. Cut chilled dough into 1/4 inch slices. Arrange, 1 inch apart on baking sheets. Use a cookie stamp to imprint the tops of half of the rounds.
3. Bake 8-11 minutes, or until cookies just begin to brown on the bottom. Cool on racks. While cookies are baking, prepare the filling. In a small mixing bowl, combine powdered sugar, butter, mint extract and 2-5 tablespoons of half-and-half. Beat at low speed until smooth, adding more half-and-half if necessary. The mixture should be thick and creamy.
4. Spread a 1/8 to 1/4 inch layer of mint filling over the bottoms of the plain cookie rounds. Top with imprinted rounds to create sandwiches; press down lightly. Refrigerate until filling is firm, about 20 minutes.
5. To prepare glaze: In a small saucepan, combine chocolate and oil. Warm over low heat, stirring occasionally, until mixture is smooth. Pour chocolate into a small deep bowl or 1-cup glass measure. Line a large baking sheet with waxed paper. Dip half of each cookie sandwich into melted chocolate, shaking off excess. Arrange sandwiches on waxed paper and refrigerate until chocolate is set. Store in an airtight container at room temperature for up to one week; freeze for longer storage.
